step_by_step_thinking: I agree that the sharpest cusp is generally observed in the upper canine. One additional point supporting this is that the occlusal surface morphology of the maxillary canine includes prominent central and distobuccal cusps, contributing significantly to their distinctive shape and sharpness compared to other teeth like pre-molars where the cusps tend to be less defined.
raw_response:
I agree that the sharpest cusp is generally observed in the upper canine. One additional point supporting this is that the occlusal surface morphology of the maxillary canine includes prominent central and distobuccal cusps, contributing significantly to their distinctive shape and sharpness compared to other teeth like pre-molars where the cusps tend to be less defined. 

Answer: A. Upper Canine.
